description: Agent asked a compound question with two sub-parts; candidate answered only the first (inventory) and ignored the second (success metrics). Judge should push_back to get the missing sub-part.

judge_input.active_question_text: You're inheriting a portfolio of ~40-50 integrations across cloud and on-prem. In your first 90 days, how would you assess, stabilize, and standardize the estate? Outline the plan, artifacts you'd produce, and success metrics.
active_question_positive_evidence: Creates an inventory and dependency map
Establishes baselines (failure rate, p95 latency) and defines SLAs/SLOs
Quantifies targets/outcomes (e.g., 30% incident reduction, MTTR < X mins)
active_question_red_flags: Vague stakeholder meetings with no technical assessment

active_question_rubric.excellent: Concrete 90-day plan with inventory, metric baselines, prioritized remediation, and quantified targets.
active_question_rubric.meets_bar: Plausible plan covering assessment and some metrics.
active_question_rubric.below_bar: High-level activity list without artifacts or metrics.

judge_input.active_question_evaluation_hint: Look for lifecycle ownership indicators and metric-driven prioritization.

active_question_signal_metadata.value: 8+ years overall experience in enterprise integration design and implementation

active_question_remaining_probes.0: What evidence and environment data would you gather in week 1?
active_question_remaining_probes.1: How would you prioritize remediation work?
active_question_remaining_probes.2: What reusable standards would you introduce to reduce future incidents?

8+ years overall experience in enterpris.signal_value: 8+ years overall experience in enterprise integration design and implementation

recent_turns.text: You're inheriting 40-50 integrations. How would you assess the estate and what success metrics would you target?

judge_input.candidate_utterance: I would start by doing an inventory of all the integrations — who owns them, what they connect, and whether they're cloud or on-prem. That way I'd have a full picture of what I'm working with.
